sections.title: What is a Subset?
points: Set A is a subset of Set B when every member of A is also in B.
We write this as A ⊂ B.

sections.title: The Empty Set
points: The empty set has no members and is written {} or Ø.
The empty set is a subset of every set.

sections.title: Counting Subsets
points: The total number of subsets of a set with n members is 2ⁿ.
Example: A = {1, 2, 4} has 3 members, so 2³ = 8 subsets.
Each member can either be inside a subset or left out.

sections.text: Important: The whole set itself is always one of its subsets.

cards.fact: A set whose members all appear inside another set.
cards.mnemonic: A subset fits inside a bigger set like a smaller box.

cards.fact: The formula 2ⁿ gives the total number of subsets.
cards.mnemonic: Every item has 2 choices: in or out.

cards.fact: We use ⊂ to say one set is inside another set.
cards.mnemonic: The shape points from smaller to larger.
